title: This astonishing $850 gaming PC contains well over $1,200 worth of parts, including $400 of 32GB DDR5 alone get a potent Steam Machine rival thanks to this budget Intel Arc desktop
summary: Get a powerful gaming PC with savings of $150 on a Intel Arc desktop, featuring a Core i5 14400F, Intel Arc B580, and 32GB DDR5 RAM.
insight: Exceptional value offered by this budget gaming PC setup.

title: Oregon will start charging service providers for undersea cables using its sea floor millions accrued over years will go toward state schools
summary: Oregon starts charging for undersea cable use, funding state schools with revenue.
insight: New revenue stream supports public education in Oregon

title: Grand Theft Auto Multiverse with real-time portals created by ambitious modder San Andreas, Vice City and GTA III all run simultaneously, with cross-game interactions
summary: A modder named Dryxio has created a groundbreaking mod that connects Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas, Vice City, and GTA III through real-time portals, allowing for seamless cross-game interactions within a single, immersive experience.
insight: Innovative modding pushes gaming boundaries with intergame connectivity.

title: Framework nearly doubles memory pricing for 32GB, 64GB Laptop 13 Pro overnight CEO says absorbing LPCAMM2 supplier hikes would put 'our ability to operate at real financial risk'
summary: The company is adjusting pre-orders for 32GB and 64GB laptops due to increased memory costs. A supplier price hike has led to a near doubling of memory prices. This change will be absorbed by the company.
insight: Supplier price hike significantly impacts laptop manufacturer's memory costs.

title: Jensen Huang argues American companies should be allowed to use Chinese AI models Nvidia CEO says backdoors connected to China are misconceptions
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang argues against restrictions on US access to Chinese AI models, advocating for open models which he believes makes AI more secure.
insight: Restricting AI access may hinder innovation and security, not enhance it.

title: Microsoft announces Xbox Backward Compatibility for PC will let gamers play classic console games on PCs and handhelds
summary: Microsoft's Xbox Backward Compatibility on PC will allow gamers to play classic Xbox games on their PCs and handheld devices.
insight: Reviving nostalgia and expanding gaming options for Xbox fans.

title: The future of USB connectivity (2026) How USB4 Version 2 and Thunderbolt 5 are bringing copper to its physical limits
summary: The USB ecosystem is evolving with USB4 Version 2 and Thunderbolt 5, pushing copper to its physical limits. These updates aim to increase speeds and capacities while reducing energy consumption. This transition will impact the way devices connect and interact.
insight: Faster speeds require innovative solutions due to copper's physical constraints

title: AMD to supply Anthropic with 2 gigawatts of Instinct MI450 GPUs will invest up to $5 billion in the Claude developer, which is already using MI355X GPUs
summary: AMD will supply Anthropic with 2 gigawatts of Instinct MI450 GPUs, worth up to $5 billion, starting with a 1-gigawatt deployment in 2027 using AMD Helios rack-scale systems. Anthropic is already utilizing MI355X GPUs. This deal is a significant investment for both parties.
insight: Accelerating AI development with massive GPU infrastructure investments

title: Data centers forecast to use 20% of US power by 2035 analysts estimate usage will rocket to 194 gigawatts, 83% more than forecast seven months ago
summary: Data centers are forecast to use 194 gigawatts of US power by 2035, an 83% increase from a previous estimate. This demand is 36% higher than a projection published just seven months prior. The growth is attributed to increased energy consumption.
insight: Data centers' energy demand is skyrocketing at alarming rates

title: Apple reportedly working on Mac leasing program in partnership with Klarna to fight RAM price increases 'Apple Upgrade' would let users finance hardware over 36 months, budget models excluded
summary: Apple is reportedly working on a leasing program called 'Apple Upgrade' that allows customers to finance hardware over 2-3 years, with options to keep, return, or upgrade devices.
insight: Addressing RAM price increases with flexible financing options.

title: AI tech companies have hidden debt worth around $1.65 trillion, report claims amount is 122% of debt reflected on the balance sheets of Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, Microsoft, and Oracle
summary: Five major tech companies have $1.65 trillion in hidden data center debt, not reflected on their balance sheets. These liabilities are listed as footnotes in their quarterly statements and will become due when the data centers start operating. This highlights the significant financial commitments made by the companies.
insight: Hidden debt masks tech giants' true financial obligations

title: Chinese modder gets GeForce RTX 4060 working in Windows 11 on Huawei Arm workstation uses modified driver borrowed from an Nvidia RTX Spark
summary: Chinese modder VoidTech successfully runs x86 Windows games on Huawei Arm workstation using a modified driver borrowed from Nvidia's RTX Spark. This achievement marks a significant breakthrough in running x86 games on Arm-based hardware. The modder's work paves the way for potential future gaming options on Arm workstations.
insight: Achieving x86 game compatibility on Arm hardware expands gaming possibilities

title: Meta to use custom AMD Instinct MI400 accelerators with 144GB of HBM4 for select workloads, report claims could dramatically reduce cost at the expense of versatility
summary: Meta is reportedly using a custom AMD Instinct MI400 accelerator with reduced HBM4 memory, 144GB, for select workloads only.
insight: Customization may lower costs, but limits versatility and adaptability.

title: Woot has slashed $600 off this RTX 5070 gaming laptop get a Gigabyte Aero X16 with 32GB of RAM and Ryzen HX 370 for just $1,399
summary: Gigabyte Aero X16 laptop comes with an RTX 5070, 32GB RAM, and 1TB SSD for $1,399.
insight: Significant price drop for high-end gaming laptop with advanced features.

title: Just $1,399 for this potent RTX 5060 Ti 16GB-powered gaming PC 32GB of DDR5 RAM and AMD's 7800X3D to help you crush the competition
summary: Save $600 on the Skytech Gaming King 95 prebuilt gaming PC with Ryzen 7 7800X3D, RTX 5060 Ti 16GB, and 32GB DDR5 RAM.
insight: High-performance gaming PC at an unbeatable price point

title: Adata chairman says DRAM shortage will last another 10 years dismisses AI bubble talk until '2040 or 2050'
summary: The DRAM shortage is expected to last another 10 years, while green power will be the world's scarcest resource. Chen, Adata's chairman, also dismissed concerns about an AI bubble until 2040 or 2050.
insight: Memory scarcity will impact global tech industry development and growth.

title: PlayStation 3 emulator adds support for ATI Radeon HD 2000, 3000, and 4000 series graphics cards on Linux 20-year-old HD 2600 crumbles, can only run Portal at 13 fps in 273p
summary: RPCS3 PlayStation 3 emulator now supports older Radeon graphics cards on Linux, allowing gamers with ATI Radeon HD 2000, 3000, and 4000 series GPUs to run the emulator. The minimum system requirements have been adjusted accordingly. However, performance may vary depending on the card's capabilities.
insight: Older graphics cards can now run PlayStation 3 emulator on Linux

title: AIS-1000 AI Surveillance Station A $3,640 NDAA and TAA-compliant AI box based on Raspberry Pi 5 and Hailo-8L
summary: The AIS-1000 AI Surveillance Station is a $3,640, NDAA and TAA-compliant device that combines a Raspberry Pi 5 with a Hailo-8L AI accelerator and a 1TB NVMe SSD, designed for high-security applications.
insight: Enhanced surveillance capabilities for secure government and critical infrastructure use.

title: Icy Electronics nRF54L15 Discovery devboard integrates a snappable nRF52820 CMSIS-DAP debugger
summary: The nRF54L15 Discovery is a compact wireless devboard with an onboard CMSIS-DAP debugger for IoT applications, featuring a snap-apart design and advanced power management.
insight: Advances IoT development with compact, snap-apart, and highly integrated design.

title: $99 Xteink X4 Pro 4.3-inch touchscreen eReader to support CrossPoint Reader open-source firmware
summary: The Xteink X4 Pro is a compact eReader with a 4.3-inch touchscreen display, 16GB storage, and WiFi/Bluetooth connectivity, available for $99. It supports various formats and has a built-in front light. The device also supports CrossPoint Reader open-source firmware.
insight: Affordable eReader option with open-source firmware support released

title: Intel lays off some data center group workers amid unit revenue growth
summary: Intel has laid off an unspecified number of Data Center Group workers as the company shifts focus to Edge AI and data center inference, continuing a multi-year reduction in force.
insight: Intel prioritizes Edge AI over traditional data center operations

title: Gig economy workers and Amazon staff are increasingly dependent on food stamps and Medicaid, according to federal report
summary: A GAO report found Amazon workers relying on food stamps and Medicaid tripled from February 2020 to September 2025.
insight: Growing reliance on government aid among gig economy workers and Amazon staff is a concern.

title: Nvidia's Jensen Huang defends Chinese AI: "Open-source models that are excellent should be used"
summary: Nvidia's Jensen Huang defends Chinese AI, emphasizing the use of open-source models.
insight: Open-source AI models gaining recognition in the global AI landscape.

title: Amazon, Meta, Microsoft, and other US tech giants are hiding $1.65 trillion in AI debt
summary: US tech giants like Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft have accumulated $3 trillion in debt, largely due to costly AI infrastructure spending, including data center leases and computing hardware.
insight: Massive AI debt threatens tech giants' long-term financial sustainability and agility.

title: Amazon orders Robocop TV series as franchise heads to Prime Video
summary: Amazon has acquired the rights to the Robocop TV series, expanding the franchise on Prime Video.
insight: Amazon expands streaming library with iconic sci-fi IP acquisition.

title: Palworld's 1.0 launch is bringing back nearly a million players
summary: Palworld 1.0 launched on July 10, attracting nearly a million players. The game peaked at 855,525 concurrent players on launch weekend and climbed to 961,867 on July 19. SteamDB figures show a significant increase in player count.
insight: Palworld's success highlights strong demand for monster-taming games.

title: Firefox 153 ESR arrives with major productivity, privacy, and enterprise upgrades
summary: Firefox 153 ESR arrives with numerous improvements, including profile management and Split View, as well as stronger privacy protections and AI features. These updates aim to enhance user productivity and experience. The new release also includes various platform and enterprise enhancements.
insight: Major Firefox upgrade boosts productivity and user privacy features

title: The Marines' new AI gun can track and target people, even though it's marketed as anti-drone
summary: The Marine Corps has selected the Bullfrog M240 robotic station, worth $6.2 million, for integration into its Light Marine Air Defense Integrated System.
insight: AI-equipped gun system can target people, not just drones.

title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 and Fold 8 Ultra debut with Flex Titanium displays and Snapdragon 8 Gen 5
summary: Samsung introduces Galaxy Z Fold 8 and Fold 8 Ultra with improved displays and processors, potentially countering Apple's rumored foldable iPhone.
insight: Samsung innovates foldable tech to maintain market lead amidst Apple competition

title: Nvidia's first Windows on Arm GeForce driver confirms RTX Spark configurations
summary: Nvidia has released its first native GeForce driver for Windows on Arm, confirming two RTX Spark configurations with high-core GPUs and CPUs. The driver targets Microsoft's Surface RTX Spark Dev Box, but allows developers to port Arm64 apps. This preparation is ahead of the RTX Spark's fall launch on various laptops and mini PCs.
insight: Nvidia prepares Windows on Arm ecosystem for high-performance gaming.

title: Data centers on track to consume 20% of US power by 2035
summary: US data centers are projected to consume 194 gigawatts of power by 2035, an 83% increase from the previous forecast, which is roughly equivalent to the output of 194 traditional nuclear reactors.
insight: Data centers' massive power demand threatens US energy infrastructure.

title: Reddit and major publishers consider blocking Google as AI search continues destroying web traffic
summary: Major publishers like Reddit and USA Today consider blocking Google due to declining referral traffic.
insight: Referral traffic collapse threatens publishers' business models and revenue streams.

title: TSMC plans up to 10% price increases on advanced and mature nodes starting in 2027
summary: TSMC plans to increase prices for semiconductor process nodes by up to 10% starting in 2027, with further hikes possible for later orders.
insight: Semiconductor price hikes impact global tech industry pricing and profitability.

title: Iran claims it destroyed Amazon's main data hub in Bahrain. Nobody has confirmed it
summary: Iran's IRGC claims it targeted Amazon's main data hub in Bahrain, but the claim has not been confirmed.
insight: Potential global cyber impact and data security concerns arise.

title: Watch Samsung unveil the Z Fold 8 and Z Flip 8 at Galaxy Unpacked
summary: Samsung is set to unveil the Galaxy Z Fold 8 and Z Flip 8, featuring significant design updates and improved display panels.
insight: Revamped design and display enhancements boost premium smartphone appeal
